name: Whowie
description: The Whowie is a fearsome creature from Australian Aboriginal mythology, particularly from the Murray River region. It is described as a giant, six-legged lizard-like monster that emerged from the ground to devour people.
appearance: Whowie is known from recorded folklore and oral tradition in Australia.
behavior: In traditional accounts, Whowie from Oceania  plays its role in the mythology and folklore of its culture.
summary: The Whowie is a fearsome creature from Australian Aboriginal mythology, particularly from the Murray River region. It is described as a giant, six-legged lizard-like monster that emerged from the ground to devour people.
fun_fact: A monster figure from Oceanic mythology.
